reason: The remediation modifies /etc/fstab to add the nodev mount option to all non-root local partitions. While the change improves security, it alters filesystem mount behavior and can cause boot failures or service disruptions if any applications rely on device nodes on those partitions. Because it is a filesystem-level change, the policy classifies it as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; Service disruption for applications expecting device nodes on non-root partitions; Compatibility issues with legacy software
